text.title: csharp-aspnet-core Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-aspnet-core as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: async void used for "fire-and-forget" notification callbacks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One unhandled exception inside async void crashes the host. Use Task-returning methods and background services instead.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In .NET Core 6+ this rarely deadlocks but causes thread-pool starvation. Force agents to await all the way through.

failure-mode.description: Repository methods return IQueryable<T> for caller composition.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: By the time the caller materializes the query, the DbContext is disposed. Always ToListAsync / ToArrayAsync inside the repo.

failure-mode.description: PagedResult<T> re-invented per project (TotalCount vs Total, PageIndex vs Page).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents ship incompatible pagination shapes. Centralize in Common/Models and reference from every handler.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Socket exhaustion. Always inject IHttpClientFactory; Roslynator rule detects the pattern.

failure-mode.description: Connection strings or JWT secrets committed in appsettings.json.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use dotnet user-secrets in dev and env vars + Key Vault in prod. Add a pre-commit secret scanner.
